{{Quote|The crystal responds to you! This is very rare indeed.|Kreia, to Meetra Surik|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
The '''Meetra Surik Crystal''' was a naturally-formed [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] that had forged a [[Force-bond/Legends|bond]] with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Meetra Surik]], resonating with [[Gender/Legends|her]] and her relationship with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. As a result, the crystal acquired its character from Surik and had the ability to augment her [[Force power/Legends|powers]].

In [[First Jedi Purge|the wake of]] the [[Jedi Civil War]], Surik, who was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] due to her participation in the [[Mandalorian Wars]], found the crystal formation within a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Crystal Cave (Dantooine)|crystal cave]] located on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] during her search for [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vrook Lamar]]. The use of the crystal as a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|focus]] ensured that the weapon could only be used by Surik as well as enhancing the other components and subsidiary crystals found in the lightsaber.

==Properties==
{{Quote|This crystal will make an excellent focus for a lightsaber.|Kreia, to Meetra Surik|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
The Meetra Surik Crystal was part of a natural [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] formation in the [[Crystal Cave (Dantooine)|Crystal Cave]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. It absorbed excess energy from beings who were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|attuned]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Upon discovery by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Meetra Surik]],<ref name="KotOR II" /> the crystal formed a [[Force-bond/Legends|bond]] with her, and as such, acquired its character and strength from Surik's own relationship with the Force. In turn, the crystal was capable of augmenting Surik's own [[Force power/Legends|powers]] and physical finesse.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> The crystal formation was glowing [[Color/Legends|green]] on the cave floor.<ref name="KotOR II" />

{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
After Surik had picked the crystal from the cave,<ref name="KotOR II" /> it was light-to-white in color and, as Surik matured in the Force, the depth of the crystal's power manifested itself by turning the glow of the crystal from translucent to a near-blinding, radiating light.<ref name="Prima" /> The use of the crystal as a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|focus]] enhanced the potency of the other components and subsidiary crystals used in the lightsaber's construction, as well as ensuring that the weapon could only be used by Surik.<ref name="KotOR II" />
{{Endgame}}

==History==
{{Quote|Yes, the crystals in this cave are unlike any I have seen. Some are warm to the touch, while others are freezing. They seem to contain energy of some sort.|[[Ahrnell]], to Meetra Surik|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
[[File:LightExilecrystal.jpg|thumb|left|70px|The crystal's light side appearance]]
In the year [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="kotorcg" /> in [[First Jedi Purge|the end of]] the [[Dark Wars]], Meetra Surik—[[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for defying the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Council's]] wishes during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]—and her companions arrived on Dantooine in search of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vrook Lamar]]. During her visit for information to [[Khoonda]], the seat of the Dantooine government, she was informed by [[Zherron]], the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Khoonda Militia]], of a problem concerning attacks from the insectoid [[Kinrath/Legends|kinrath]] on local settlers. Surik offered to eliminate the kinrath hive that Zherron believed to be located in a cave in the nearby [[Khoonda plains]].<ref name="KotOR II" />

Surik and two of her companions arrived at the crystal cave, and the group fought their way through the kinrath hive in the tunnels to the last cavern, the location of the nest. After Surik defeated the kinrath matriarch and her hatchlings, [[Kreia]], one of her companions and informal Jedi Master, informed her that the cavern was a Force-sensitive location in which crystals absorbed and reflected Force energy. As Surik searched for Force-infused crystals to harvest for eventual placement in a lightsaber, she came upon the glowing crystal formation in which she found the crystal that bound itself to her, a rare phenomenon, according to Kreia. Throughout the duration of the journey, as Surik's connection to the Force developed and deepened, Kreia assisted Surik in refocusing the crystal's properties to reflect Surik's burgeoning relationship with the Force.<ref name="KotOR II" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:DarkExilecrystal.jpg|thumb|right|70px|The crystal's dark side appearance]]
Meetra Surik crystal was created for [[Obsidian Entertainment]]'s [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''.<ref name="KotOR II" /> In the game, the Jedi Exile's name is chosen by the player, and the crystal gains the name accordingly.<ref name="KotOR IIc">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' dialogue file, StrRef 110092 – 110631: "''<FullName> Crystal''"</ref> In the [[2011]] novel ''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'', the name of the Jedi Exile was confirmed to be Meetra Surik.<ref name="revan" />

During gameplay, the player character has the choice to follow either the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] or [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] path during quests, and this may result in a shift in the Force alignment of the character. The characteristics of the Meetra Surik crystal will reflect these changes in alignment, as well. Depending on the player character's alignment, the crystal may have a color of light-to-white, gray, or, dark-to-black as well as the brightness changing between blinding, intense, brilliant, dim, or opaque. Furthermore, there will be changes in the bonuses applied to the player's charisma, dexterity, and strength, in addition to the amount of damage dealt to an opponent. In total, there are forty-seven variations of the crystal for color, abilities, and glow available to the player, but only one variation will be in use at any given time.<ref name="Prima" />
